structural_non_business_differences: Vercel route additionally validates an HTTP Authorization bearer header (isAuthorized()) -- not applicable to a CLI invocation, not a business-logic difference
run_id prefix differs: 'openserp-cron-' (Vercel) vs 'openserp-github-cron-' (native) -- cosmetic/traceability only

permissions.finding: openserp-github-native-ingestion.yml declares no top-level 'permissions:' key -- it implicitly inherits the repository's default Actions token permissions, which is not verifiable from the workflow file alone.
permissions.note: All 5 secrets already exist and are already used successfully by 6/6 real scheduled runs -- no gap in credential availability.
permissions.extra_flagged_item: Two steps explicitly commented 'TEMPORARY diagnostic... Remove once the SUPABASE_URL issue is confirmed/resolved' still read SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY beyond the single ingestion step, despite the underlying issue being demonstrably resolved (6/6 successful runs). Not a new secret, not a value leak, but unnecessary residual access surface.

run_history_sample.workflow_dispatch_runs_conclusion: 8/12 success, 4/12 failure (all 4 predate schedule activation, pre-go-live validation/debug phase on 2026-07-19)

cadence.explanation: GitHub documents that scheduled workflow runs are best-effort and may be delayed or dropped under platform load, with reduced frequency on lower-activity repositories -- this is expected platform behavior for the 'schedule' trigger, not a defect in this workflow's own configuration.

github_actions.billing_api_note: A read-only call to the repo Actions-permissions API was declined by the user during this audit; plan/quota comparison against actual GitHub billing was not attempted afterward to respect that instruction. Only observed run-minutes are reported here, no invented quota figures.

openserp_docker.note: ephemeral per-run container, no persistent hosting cost

vercel.note: no longer receiving scheduled ingestion traffic; route remains callable manually for fallback/debug only, 0 scheduled invocations observed since cutover

monthly_projection.caveat: extrapolated from a 16h sample only; not a verified billing figure

shared_lock_and_writes.evidence: 3 distinct ingestion_run_id values found across the 60 most recent discovery_candidates rows, all with the 'openserp-github-cron-' prefix used only by the native script; 0 rows with the Vercel route's 'openserp-cron-' prefix in this sample.

risks.recommendation: Add permissions: contents: read (or narrower) at workflow level

risks.risk: Two residual TEMPORARY diagnostic steps still read Supabase secrets beyond what current operation requires
risks.recommendation: Remove now that the original SUPABASE_URL issue is confirmed resolved (6/6 successful runs)

risks.risk: No job-level timeout-minutes set (GitHub default is 360 minutes)
risks.recommendation: Add an explicit timeout-minutes cap (e.g. 10) -- the DB lease already self-heals at the business-logic level, this would bound wasted Actions runner time on a genuine hang

risks.risk: Schedule cadence runs ~5.3x slower than nominal (GitHub platform throttling on a low-activity repo)
risks.recommendation: Document as an accepted, expected characteristic; do not treat as a regression

risks.risk: GitHub Actions billing/quota not independently verified via API in this audit
risks.recommendation: Check via repository Settings UI in a future pass if precise quota comparison is needed

success_criteria_check.no_unnecessary_secret_or_git_content_acc: partially -- two residual diagnostic steps flagged
success_criteria_check.costs_observed_and_documented: partially -- billing API not queried, run-minute observation only
